raw_consumer_statement: On XX/XX/XXXX, my bank account was hacked, along with my XXXX account and my Barclay Bluegreen Mastercard. The hacker also requested online statements from my Mastercard. Then hacker attempted to pay off the balance on my Bluegreen card, but because I didn't recognize the payment, I had the bank reject the payment. Then somehow a new card was issued in my name on my account and several transactions posted to this new account. When I was notified by email of this new card and the transactions, I called Bluegreen XXXX and reported the fraudulent transactions. They removed those transactions and that card was closed, but another card was issued and more transactions posted to that account. I again called Bluegreen Mastercard and reported those transactions as fraudulent. I had them close that account. Every time I talk to the fraud department with Bluegreen Mastercard they assure me that the account is closed. There is a balance of {$120.00} on the account, all of that from the fraudulent transactions and a late payment fee. I have talked to several different people over a dozen times. In the past two weeks I have received 2 calls a day from Barclay card informing me that my {$59.00} payment is overdue. I've talked to them in the past two weeks, but nothing ever seems to get resolved. I don't think that I should have to pay off a balance that I am not responsible for.
